vision: A comprehensive travel companion app designed specifically for solo travelers to plan adventures, stay safe, discover destinations, and connect with a community of fellow independent explorers.

target_audience.primary: Solo travelers who plan and manage their own adventures independently
secondary: Safety-conscious travelers who need check-in and location sharing features
Travel enthusiasts who want to document and share their journeys
Backpackers and budget travelers seeking destination recommendations
Female solo travelers prioritizing safety features
Digital nomads who need offline access and trip organization tools

phases.name: Foundation & Reliability
phases.description: Complete MVP foundation, fix critical gaps, ensure rock-solid offline functionality and performance - directly addressing top competitor pain points around app instability, sync issues, and offline failures that plague TripIt, Wanderlog, and Sygic.

milestones.title: Complete Profile Management UI
milestones.description: Users can create, edit, and manage their profiles with photos, bio, travel preferences, and stats

milestones.title: Rock-Solid Offline Experience
milestones.description: All core features work perfectly without connectivity - addresses #1 competitor pain point about offline functionality failures

milestones.title: Performance & Reliability Excellence
milestones.description: App handles large trip datasets smoothly, no crashes or lag - addresses Wanderlog and Sygic performance complaints

milestones.title: Privacy-First Safety Suite
milestones.description: Complete safety features with granular privacy controls - addresses Polarsteps and Couchsurfing privacy concerns

milestones.title: Smart Notification System
milestones.description: Context-aware check-in reminders and safety alerts without notification spam - addresses TripIt notification overload complaints

phases.name: Safety & Community
phases.description: Launch enhanced safety features specifically for solo travelers (especially women) and community connections - addressing major market gaps around integrated safety and solo female traveler needs that competitors like Couchsurfing fail to address.

milestones.title: Women-First Safety Features
milestones.description: Enhanced safety suite designed specifically for solo female travelers - addresses Couchsurfing's #1 critical safety gap

milestones.title: Traveler Community Launch
milestones.description: Verified community for solo travelers to connect, share tips, and find travel buddies - addresses isolation pain point

milestones.title: Local Emergency Intelligence
milestones.description: Comprehensive local safety information, emergency contacts, and embassy details for every destination

milestones.title: Integrated Safety Ecosystem
milestones.description: All safety features work together seamlessly - check-ins, location sharing, emergency alerts, and trusted contacts in one place

phases.name: Smart Planning & Discovery
phases.description: Add AI-powered recommendations, advanced discovery, and content sharing - differentiating through intelligent, personalized travel planning while avoiding AI 'nonsense' problems reported by Wanderlog users.

milestones.title: AI-Powered Recommendations
milestones.description: Personalized destination and activity recommendations using AI - conservative, transparent approach avoids Wanderlog's AI accuracy issues

milestones.title: Solo-Friendly Discovery Engine
milestones.description: Find destinations specifically rated for solo travel with safety scores and community insights

milestones.title: Content & Trip Sharing
milestones.description: Share trips and journals publicly or with select contacts - addresses TripIt's paywall complaint by making sharing free

milestones.title: Advanced Search & Filtering
milestones.description: Powerful search across trips, journals, and destinations with smart filters - addresses Wanderlog's search problems

phases.name: Platform Enhancement
phases.description: Add booking integrations, multi-language support, and collaborative features - transforming from planning tool to complete travel platform while maintaining focus on solo travelers.

milestones.title: Booking Integration
milestones.description: Seamless booking integration for accommodations, transportation, and activities within planned itineraries

milestones.title: Global Accessibility
milestones.description: Multi-language support and localization for major international markets

milestones.title: Collaborative Planning
milestones.description: Temporary group collaboration for specific trip segments - addresses Wanderlog's collaboration requirement limitation

milestones.title: Data Portability
milestones.description: Trip export/import capabilities and travel book generation - addresses user desire to preserve and share travel memories

phases.name: Analytics & Optimization
phases.description: Launch travel analytics, performance optimizations, and advanced features for power users - focusing on efficiency and insights while maintaining app simplicity.

milestones.title: Travel Analytics Dashboard
milestones.description: Comprehensive travel statistics, spending insights, and journey visualization

milestones.title: Performance Optimization
milestones.description: Further optimizations for battery efficiency and app responsiveness - addresses Polarsteps battery drain complaints

milestones.title: Power User Features
milestones.description: Advanced features for frequent travelers including bulk operations, templates, and automation

features.title: Complete Profile Management UI
features.description: Finish the presentation layer for user profiles including profile creation, editing, photo uploads, bio management, travel preferences, and travel statistics display. The domain and data layers are complete - only UI implementation needed.
features.rationale: User profiles are fundamental for community features and personalization. Competitor apps like Couchsurfing show profiles are critical for community trust, but their verification is inadequate. We need robust profiles with proper verification as foundation for community features.
acceptance_criteria: Users can create profiles with display name, photo, bio, and travel preferences
Profile editing allows updating all fields with real-time validation
Profile photos upload and display correctly with proper cropping
Travel statistics (countries visited, trips completed) display on profile
Profile data persists across app restarts and syncs with backend
user_stories: As a solo traveler, I want to create a profile with my travel history so that other travelers can learn about my experience level
As a user, I want to update my profile photo and bio to reflect my current travel journey
As a community member, I want to see other travelers' profiles to understand their travel background and preferences

features.title: Robust Offline-First Architecture Completion
features.description: Complete the offline-first architecture implementation by fixing commented-out offline-aware repository registrations, implementing proper user context for offline data, and ensuring all core features (viewing trips, journaling, check-ins) work flawlessly without connectivity.
features.rationale: This is the #1 complaint across competitors: TripIt, Wanderlog, and Sygic all fail when offline. For solo travelers visiting remote areas, offline functionality isn't a luxury - it's essential. This directly addresses competitor pain points pain-1-1, pain-2-2, and pain-4-3.
acceptance_criteria: All trip data (itineraries, journals, check-ins) accessible without network connection
Users can create and edit journal entries while offline
Safety check-ins can be triggered and recorded while offline
Offline data syncs seamlessly and automatically when connection restored
Sync conflict resolution handles multiple offline edits correctly
App indicates offline status clearly to users
No data loss when app closed/reopened while offline
user_stories: As a solo traveler in remote areas, I want to access my trip plans without internet so I can navigate even with poor connectivity
As a backpacker, I want to journal my experiences offline and sync later when I find WiFi
As a safety-conscious traveler, I want to trigger check-ins while offline in areas with no cell service

features.title: Intelligent Sync & Conflict Resolution
features.description: Implement comprehensive sync conflict resolution system for handling concurrent edits from multiple devices, resolving offline changes, and preventing data loss during sync operations. Includes pending changes sync for auth manager.
features.rationale: TripIt users report constant reconnection needs and data not saving locally (pain-1-1). Wanderlog has connection failures requiring restarts (pain-2-2). Robust sync with conflict resolution prevents these frustrating data loss issues and builds user trust.
acceptance_criteria: Automatic sync triggers when connectivity restored after offline period
Conflict resolution detects and resolves concurrent edits intelligently
Users can manually choose between conflicting versions when automatic resolution isn't possible
Sync progress indicators show current sync status
No data loss occurs during sync interruptions or failures
Pending auth changes sync properly when connection available
Last-synced timestamp visible to users
user_stories: As a traveler using multiple devices, I want my changes to sync across devices so I always have the latest version of my trip
As a user who edits trips offline, I want my offline changes to merge correctly with online changes when I reconnect
As a user, I want to see sync status so I know when my data is safely backed up

features.title: Performance Optimization for Large Datasets
features.description: Optimize app performance to handle large trip datasets with multiple destinations, long itineraries, extensive journals, and media attachments without lag or crashes. Implements lazy loading, efficient caching, and optimized Riverpod state management.
features.rationale: Wanderlog users report significant lag when adding multiple items to itineraries (pain-2-1). Sygic crashes near end of trips causing data loss (pain-4-1). Performance is a top complaint across competitors - smooth performance even with complex trips is a major differentiator.
acceptance_criteria: App handles trips with 50+ itinerary items without lag
Scrolling through long journals with multiple photos remains smooth
Trip list loads instantly even with 100+ trips
Search and filter operations complete in under 1 second
Memory usage remains stable during extended sessions
No crashes occur when navigating between screens with large datasets
Cached data provider fully implements efficient trip caching
user_stories: As a long-term traveler with extensive trip history, I want the app to remain fast and responsive even with many trips
As a planner of complex multi-week itineraries, I want to add and edit many items without the app becoming sluggish
As a user with a large journal collection, I want to search and browse my entries quickly

features.title: Crash Prevention & Error Handling
features.description: Implement comprehensive error handling, auto-save mechanisms, and crash prevention. Fix CloudWatch monitoring dependency issues, add proper error boundaries, and ensure graceful degradation when features fail.
features.rationale: Sygic's app crashes (pain-4-1) cause data loss and user frustration. TripIt's instability (pain-1-1) drives users to competitors. Rock-solid reliability with proper error handling builds trust and prevents the data loss nightmares users report with competitor apps.
acceptance_criteria: App auto-saves all user input every 30 seconds to prevent data loss
Proper error messages display when operations fail instead of silent failures
App gracefully handles network errors without crashing
CloudWatch monitoring packages properly integrated for crash tracking
Error boundaries prevent individual component failures from crashing entire app
Users can recover from errors without data loss
Crash reporting (Sentry) properly configured and actionable
user_stories: As a user, I want my work to be automatically saved so I don't lose data if the app crashes
As a user, I want clear error messages when something goes wrong instead of the app just not working
As a user, I want the app to remain stable even when individual features have problems

features.title: Granular Privacy Controls
features.description: Implement comprehensive privacy controls allowing users to set visibility levels for trips, journal entries, and location sharing. Options include: public, community-only, friends-only, private, and custom selected contacts. Control who sees what aspects of travels.
features.rationale: Polarsteps lacks hide function for privacy (pain-3-3). Couchsurfing profiles have inadequate privacy controls (pain-5-1). Privacy is a top concern for solo travelers. Granular controls address market gap #3 and differentiate from competitors with weak privacy options.
acceptance_criteria: Users can set trip visibility to public, community, friends, or private
Individual journal entries can have different privacy levels
Location sharing allows selecting specific trusted contacts only
Profile information (location, current trip) can be hidden
Privacy settings sync across devices
Default privacy levels configurable in user preferences
Clear indicators show current privacy level for each piece of content
user_stories: As a solo traveler, I want to share my trip with friends but keep it private from the general public
As a female traveler, I want to control exactly who can see my current location for safety
As a user, I want to keep some journal entries private while sharing others

features.title: Background Check-In Service Integration
features.description: Complete the background check-in service implementation by integrating with SafetyRepository, implementing configurable check-in intervals, and ensuring reliable background execution on both iOS and Android despite OS limitations.
features.rationale: Safety apps like bSafe and Kitestring work but are single-purpose (pain-6-1). Integrating safety into the travel app itself means higher adoption and regular use during trips rather than being forgotten as a separate app.
acceptance_criteria: Background check-in prompts trigger at configurable intervals
Check-ins work reliably even when app is in background
Missed check-ins trigger emergency contact notifications
Check-in history logged and visible to user
Service respects OS background limitations and works on both iOS and Android
Battery-efficient implementation doesn't drain power
Check-in settings customizable per trip
user_stories: As a solo traveler in remote areas, I want automated check-ins so my contacts know I'm safe without me constantly remembering to check in
As a safety-conscious user, I want my emergency contacts alerted if I miss a check-in
As a traveler, I want check-in intervals that match my trip's risk level

features.title: Smart Notification System
features.description: Implement context-aware notification system with smart batching, granular user controls, and intelligent routing. Fix TODO for notification payload navigation to appropriate screens. Avoid notification overload while ensuring critical alerts come through.
features.rationale: TripIt users complain about notification overload and incorrect information (pain-1-3). Polarsteps shifted to social focus away from core purpose (pain-3-1). Smart, controlled notifications that respect user preferences address these complaints.
acceptance_criteria: Users can choose exactly which notification types they receive
Non-critical notifications batched together to reduce frequency
Check-in reminders time intelligently based on user's location and activity
Tapping notifications navigates to relevant screen (addresses TODO)
Emergency alerts bypass batching and notify immediately
Quiet hours available for non-urgent notifications
Notification preferences sync across devices
user_stories: As a user, I want to control which notifications I receive so I'm not overwhelmed
As a traveler, I want check-in reminders at appropriate times, not random intervals
As a user, I want critical safety alerts to come through immediately but less urgent updates to be batched

features.title: Enhanced Female Traveler Safety Suite
features.description: Develop specialized safety features for solo female travelers including women-only community spaces, enhanced verification for women seeking meetups, safety ratings for destinations from female perspective, and emergency features specifically designed for women's safety concerns.
features.rationale: Couchsurfing's #1 critical issue is inadequate safety for solo female travelers (pain-5-2, pain-5-3). This is a major market gap with high opportunity size. Creating women-first safety features addresses a critical unmet need and builds immense trust.
acceptance_criteria: Women-only community forums and discussion spaces
Enhanced verification process for female travelers including optional video verification
Destination safety ratings specifically for solo women with factors like lighting, harassment reports, women-friendly accommodations
Emergency quick-action buttons for different threat levels
Women-only matching for travel buddy connections
Trusted female contact network for emergency situations
user_stories: As a solo female traveler, I want to connect with other women travelers for safety tips and meetups
As a woman traveling alone, I want destination safety information from a female perspective
As a female traveler, I want enhanced safety features that address my specific concerns

features.title: Comprehensive User Verification System
features.description: Implement multi-tiered user verification system including email verification, phone verification, optional photo ID verification, social media linking, and community vouching. Verification badges visible on profiles to build trust in community interactions.
features.rationale: Couchsurfing's fake profiles and inadequate vetting cause safety concerns (pain-5-1, pain-5-3). Building trust through transparent verification makes community features safe rather than risky. Multiple verification signals provide layered trust.
acceptance_criteria: Email verification required for basic account access
Phone verification available for enhanced trust
Optional ID verification for highest trust level
Social media linking shows authenticity
Community references/vouching system from other travelers
Verification badges clearly displayed on profiles
Users can filter by verification level in community features
Verification status factors into safety scoring for meetups
user_stories: As a community member, I want to see verification badges so I know who is trustworthy
As a user looking for travel buddies, I want to filter for verified members only
As a new user, I want to verify my identity so others feel comfortable connecting with me

features.title: Solo Traveler Community Features
features.description: Build community features including discussion forums by destination and topic, traveler matching based on interests and upcoming trips, peer-to-peer messaging, and location-based community connections. Focus on meaningful connections, not just social features.
features.rationale: One of the primary pain points is lack of community and connection with fellow solo travelers. Polarsteps shifted to photo sharing (pain-3-1) but users want storytelling and connection. Safe, verified community addresses isolation pain point.
acceptance_criteria: Discussion forums organized by destination and travel topics
Traveler matching based on upcoming trips, interests, and travel style
In-app messaging between connected travelers
Location-based community discovery (find nearby verified travelers)
Community guidelines and moderation system
Block and report features for safety
Success stories and connection highlights
user_stories: As a solo traveler, I want to find other travelers in my destination to meet for safety and companionship
As a user planning a trip, I want to connect with others who have been there for tips
As a solo traveler, I want to find travel buddies for specific activities while maintaining safety

features.title: Public Trip & Journal Sharing
features.description: Complete the social sharing and public trip features in progress. Allow users to publish trip itineraries and journals publicly with shareable links, embed on websites, and share to social media. Platform-specific availability checks and download capabilities implemented.
features.rationale: TripIt locks sharing behind Pro subscription (pain-1-4). Users want to share experiences but apps either lock it behind paywalls or push to social. Making sharing free with proper controls addresses the goal of documenting and sharing.
acceptance_criteria: Users can publish entire trips or individual journal entries publicly
Shareable web links for public trips work without app installation
Embeddable trip widgets for personal blogs/websites
Social media sharing with custom preview images
Public trips respect user's chosen privacy settings
Comment system on public trips for community engagement
Platform-specific checks enable/disable sharing features appropriately
Download functionality for trip data (addresses TODO in social_sharing_service_impl)
user_stories: As a travel enthusiast, I want to share my trip journal with friends and family via a web link
As a blogger, I want to embed my SoloAdventurer trips on my travel blog
As a user, I want to share journal entries to social media to inspire others

features.title: Local Safety & Emergency Intelligence
features.description: Create comprehensive local safety information database including emergency services contacts (police, ambulance, fire), embassy and consulate information, local emergency numbers, hospital locations, safe transportation options, and area-specific safety alerts for destinations worldwide.
features.rationale: Safety is the #1 concern for solo travelers. Safety apps exist but are separate (pain-6-1). Integrating local safety intelligence directly into the travel app means travelers have critical info at their fingertips without switching apps during emergencies.
acceptance_criteria: Emergency contacts for every country (police, ambulance, fire)
Embassy and consulate information with locations and contact details
Hospital and clinic locations with ratings and emergency capabilities
Local emergency numbers displayed based on user's current location
Area-specific safety alerts and travel warnings
Safe transportation options and taxi company recommendations
Offline access to all emergency information
Quick-access emergency button on every screen
user_stories: As a solo traveler in a new country, I want quick access to local emergency numbers
As a traveler abroad, I want my embassy's contact info and location readily available
As a user in an emergency, I want to find the nearest hospital without searching multiple apps

features.title: Integrated Safety Dashboard
features.description: Create unified safety dashboard bringing together all safety features: check-in status, location sharing settings, emergency contacts, current location safety info, recent activity log, and quick access to emergency features. Central hub for safety management.
features.rationale: Safety apps are single-purpose and adoption is low (pain-6-1, pain-6-3). Integrating all safety features into one cohesive dashboard within the travel app increases usage and ensures safety is part of the travel routine.
acceptance_criteria: Unified dashboard shows all safety features at a glance
Check-in status with next scheduled check-in timer
Current location sharing settings and which contacts have access
Quick toggle for location sharing on/off
Emergency contacts with one-tap call/message
Current location safety score and emergency info
Recent activity log for safety-related actions
Emergency mode button for immediate crisis response
user_stories: As a solo traveler, I want one place to manage all my safety settings and features
As a user, I want to quickly see my check-in status and who can see my location
As a safety-conscious traveler, I want emergency features accessible with one tap

features.title: AI-Powered Travel Recommendations
features.description: Implement AI-powered destination and activity recommendations based on user's travel history, preferences, budget, and solo travel style. Conservative, transparent approach with clear distinction between user data and AI suggestions. Avoid 'nonsense' or false information problems reported with Wanderlog's AI (pain-2-6).
features.rationale: AI recommendations are mentioned in discovery gaps. However, Wanderlog's AI generates 'nonsense' and 'false' information (pain-2-6). The opportunity is providing genuinely useful, conservative AI recommendations that users can trust.
acceptance_criteria: AI recommendations based on user's travel history and stated preferences
Solo-safety scoring integrated into recommendations
Clear distinction between AI suggestions and user-created content
Source citations for AI-provided information
Users can report incorrect AI suggestions for improvement
Opt-in AI features (not forced on users)
Recommendations include budget estimates and seasonal considerations
AI suggests destinations matching user's interests and past trips
user_stories: As a solo traveler, I want AI suggestions for destinations similar to ones I've loved
As a planner, I want activity recommendations that match my interests and budget
As a user, I want to trust AI recommendations by seeing sources and being able to flag issues

features.title: Solo-Friendly Destination Discovery
features.description: Build comprehensive destination discovery engine focused on solo travel. Includes solo-safety scores, solo-friendly accommodation ratings, activities good for solo travelers, community tips from other solo travelers, and seasonal considerations.
features.rationale: Users struggle finding solo-friendly destinations (pain point in discovery). Existing discovery platforms aren't solo-specific. Solo-focused discovery with community insights addresses this gap and differentiates from generic travel apps.
acceptance_criteria: Destination search with solo-specific filters (safety, walkability, social atmosphere)
Solo-safety scores based on crime, women's safety, political stability
Solo-friendly accommodations highlighted (hostels, single rooms, social hotels)
Activities rated for solo suitability (group tours, solo dining options)
Community tips from solo travelers who've visited
Seasonal considerations (weather, peak seasons, costs)
Saved destinations and bucket list features
Integration with trip planning to add discovered destinations
user_stories: As a solo traveler, I want to find destinations that are safe and welcoming for people traveling alone
As a planner, I want to see solo-safety scores before choosing a destination
As a user, I want tips from other solo travelers about their experiences in destinations I'm considering

features.title: Advanced Search & Filtering
features.description: Implement powerful search across trips, journals, and destinations with advanced filters including date ranges, locations, tags, trip status, privacy levels, and full-text search. Optimize journal search with tag filtering performance improvements.
features.rationale: Wanderlog has search functionality issues on both web and mobile (pain-2-4). TODO in code indicates journal search optimization needed. Robust search that actually works is a basic differentiator when competitors struggle with this.
acceptance_criteria: Full-text search across trips, journals, and destinations
Filter by date range, location, tags, trip status
Saved search queries for repeated searches
Search results ranked by relevance with highlighted matches
Advanced search UI with filter combination logic (AND/OR)
Optimized tag filtering for journals (addresses TODO performance issue)
Search works offline with cached data
Search history for quick access to previous searches
user_stories: As a user with many trips, I want to search across all my trips to find specific information
As a journal keeper, I want to filter my journal entries by tags, dates, and locations
As a planner, I want to search for destinations I've saved or researched previously

features.title: Timeline & Memory Filtering
features.description: Implement comprehensive timeline filtering for trip memories, journal entries, and travel history. Filter by date ranges, trip types, emotions/moods, activities, companions, and more. Address TODO for timeline filtering implementation.
features.rationale: TODO in memory_timeline_screen.dart indicates timeline filtering is needed. Polarsteps users miss storytelling focus (pain-3-1). Rich timeline with filtering enables meaningful reflection on travel experiences beyond just photos.
acceptance_criteria: Timeline view of all travel experiences chronologically
Filter by date range, trip type, activities, emotions
Mood tracking for journal entries with mood-based filtering
Visual timeline with photos and key events
Highlights and favorites section
On this day feature showing past trips from same date
Exportable timeline summaries (for travel books)
user_stories: As a nostalgic traveler, I want to see a timeline of my travel memories filtered by mood or activity
As a journal keeper, I want to review my past trips filtered by season or trip type
As a user, I want to see 'on this day' memories from past years

features.title: Journal Editing & Management
features.description: Complete journal editing features including rich text editing, photo management, video support, tagging, and organization. Address TODO for journal editing screen navigation and implement comprehensive journal management capabilities.
features.rationale: TODO in journal_entry_detail_screen.dart indicates edit navigation is incomplete. Journaling is a core feature mentioned in discovery as complete, but editing functionality gaps remain. Full journal editing completes the core journaling feature set.
acceptance_criteria: Rich text editor with formatting options (bold, italic, headings, lists)
Photo and video management within journal entries
Location tagging and automatic location detection
Mood and activity tagging for entries
Journal entry templates for different types of entries
Draft saving and auto-save during editing
Entry organization with folders and tags
Bulk operations (move, delete, export multiple entries)
user_stories: As a journal keeper, I want to edit my journal entries with rich text formatting
As a user, I want to add photos and videos to my journal entries
As a writer, I want to save drafts and work on entries over time

features.title: Booking Integration
features.description: Integrate booking capabilities for flights, accommodations (hotels, hostels), transportation (trains, buses, rental cars), and activities. Partners with booking platforms or builds direct integrations. Adds booked items directly to trip itineraries.
features.rationale: Mentioned in discovery gaps. TripIt's automatic import creates double-bookings (pain-1-5). Manual booking integration with user control avoids this while providing convenience. Keeps users in app rather than switching to booking sites.
acceptance_criteria: Search and book accommodations integrated into trip planning
Flight search and booking with automatic itinerary population
Transportation booking (trains, buses, rental cars)
Activity and tour booking with solo-friendly options highlighted
Booked items automatically added to trip itinerary
Price comparison across providers
Booking confirmations stored in document management
User maintains full manual control (no automatic imports that cause errors)
user_stories: As a planner, I want to search and book accommodations within SoloAdventurer without switching apps
As a user, I want my booked flights automatically added to my trip itinerary
As a solo traveler, I want to see solo-friendly activities I can book directly

features.title: Multi-Language Support & Localization
features.description: Implement multi-language support for major international languages including app UI translation, localized content for destinations, and culturally appropriate recommendations. Makes app accessible to global solo travel community.
features.rationale: Listed in discovery gaps. Solo travelers come from all over the world. English-only limits market. Multi-language support expands addressable market and improves user experience for non-English speakers.
acceptance_criteria: App UI available in 5+ major languages (Spanish, French, German, Chinese, Japanese)
Easy language switching within app
Destination information available in user's preferred language
Community features support multiple languages with translation
Date, time, and number formats localized by region
Currency conversion and display in user's preferred currency
Right-to-left language support where appropriate
Community moderation across languages
user_stories: As a non-English speaker, I want the app interface in my native language
As a global traveler, I want destination information in languages I understand
As a community member, I want to connect with travelers who speak different languages

features.title: Collaborative Trip Planning
features.description: Enable temporary collaboration on trips for when solo travelers join groups or meet up with others. Allows sharing trip editing access for specific trips, collaborative itinerary building, and shared expense tracking. Collaboration ends when trip completes.
features.rationale: Wanderlog requires collaborators to also have the app (pain-2-5). Solo travelers sometimes join temporary groups. Enabling collaboration without requiring app installation addresses solo travelers' unique collaboration needs.
acceptance_criteria: Invite collaborators via email or web link (no app required for basic viewing)
Granular permissions (view only, edit, full access)
Collaborative itinerary editing with real-time updates
Shared expense tracking for group trip segments
Collaboration expires when trip marked complete
Activity log shows who made what changes
Comment threads on itinerary items for planning discussions
Collaborators can be removed at any time
user_stories: As a solo traveler joining a group tour, I want to collaborate on the itinerary with others
As a traveler meeting friends abroad, I want to share trip planning temporarily
As a user, I want to track shared expenses when I'm traveling with others temporarily

features.title: Trip Export & Import
features.description: Implement trip export and import capabilities allowing users to export their trips in standard formats (JSON, PDF), backup their data, and import trips from other travel apps. Provides data portability and peace of mind.
features.rationale: Listed in discovery gaps. Users want control over their data. Export/import capabilities address concerns about vendor lock-in and provide backup options. PDF export creates shareable itineraries.
acceptance_criteria: Export trips to JSON format for data portability
Export trips to PDF for sharing and printing
Export journal entries with photos
Import trips from standard formats or other apps
Bulk export of all user data
Scheduled automatic backups to cloud storage
Export options include selectable data (trips only, journals only, everything)
user_stories: As a user, I want to export my trips as PDFs to share with friends who don't use the app
As a data-conscious user, I want regular backups of all my travel data
As a user switching from another app, I want to import my existing trip data

features.title: Travel Book Generation
features.description: Generate beautiful travel books from trips and journals similar to Polarsteps' printed books feature. Users can compile their trips, journal entries, and photos into professionally designed books for ordering as printed copies or downloading as digital PDFs.
features.rationale: Polarsteps' travel books are a popular feature (mentioned in strengths). Discovery gaps mention trip export. Travel books are a natural extension of the journaling feature and provide physical mementos of solo adventures.
acceptance_criteria: Automatic book layout from trip data and journal entries
Multiple book templates and styles
Photo integration with automatic layout optimization
Preview book before ordering
Order printed copies through print-on-demand service
Download as high-quality PDF for self-printing
Customizable cover photos and text
Book includes maps showing travel routes
user_stories: As a sentimental traveler, I want to turn my trip journal and photos into a printed travel book
As a user, I want to download a professional PDF of my trip to share digitally
As a gift-giver, I want to order printed copies of my adventures for family and friends

features.title: Travel Analytics Dashboard
features.description: Create comprehensive travel analytics showing countries visited, miles traveled, money spent, time spent traveling, favorite destinations, travel patterns, and visualizations of travel history. Helps solo travelers understand their travel habits and growth.
features.rationale: Listed in discovery gaps. Travelers love seeing their travel stats (Polarsteps strength). Analytics provide insights, motivation, and fun visualization of solo travel achievements.
acceptance_criteria: Statistics dashboard showing countries, cities, trips, miles traveled
Spending analytics by category and destination
Travel timeline visualization
Map showing all visited locations with routes
Favorite destinations based on journal sentiment and ratings
Travel patterns (seasons, trip lengths, types of destinations)
Year-over-year travel comparisons
Shareable summary graphics for social media
user_stories: As a long-term traveler, I want to see comprehensive statistics about my travels
As a budget-conscious traveler, I want analytics on my spending patterns
As a user, I want visual representations of my travel history to share with others

features.title: Battery Efficiency Optimization
features.description: Further optimize battery efficiency for GPS tracking and background services. Implement smart tracking that activates only when moving, configurable update intervals, battery usage transparency, and power management options.
features.rationale: Polarsteps users report battery drain despite claims of minimal usage (pain-3-2). For safety check-ins and location sharing to work, they must not kill users' batteries. Battery efficiency is critical for adoption.
acceptance_criteria: GPS tracking activates only when user is moving (stationary detection)
Configurable update intervals to balance accuracy and battery
Battery usage statistics visible to users
Low-power mode for extended trips
Background service optimization to minimize wakelocks
Smart pre-caching of data during WiFi to reduce cellular data and battery
Battery usage < 5% per day during normal use
User notifications if app is using excessive battery
user_stories: As a user, I want location tracking that doesn't drain my battery during long trips
As a traveler, I want to see exactly how much battery the app is using
As a safety-conscious user, I want a low-power mode that still tracks my location for check-ins

features.title: Power User Features
features.description: Implement advanced features for frequent and power users including bulk operations, trip templates, custom workflows, keyboard shortcuts (tablets), API access for developers, and automation features for repetitive tasks.
features.rationale: Frequent solo travelers and digital nomads use the app extensively. Power user features increase engagement and loyalty for the most active users who often become community leaders and advocates.
acceptance_criteria: Bulk editing, tagging, and operations on multiple items
Trip templates for common trip types (weekend getaway, backpacking trip, etc.)
Custom workflows for trip planning routines
API access for developers to build on SoloAdventurer data
Automation rules (auto-tag, auto-categorize)
Advanced keyboard shortcuts for tablet users
Customizable home screen layout
Export of all data in machine-readable formats
user_stories: As a frequent traveler, I want to use trip templates to quickly plan similar types of trips
As a power user, I want to perform bulk operations instead of editing items one by one
As a developer, I want API access to build custom tools using my travel data
